source.title: How to attack CCTV/Cash on Clubhouse via Rafters
source.notes: Single-site walkthrough for the CCTV/Cash / Rafters site (same site as clubhouse_defense.json's 'cctv-cash'), built around clearing Rafters as the key to the whole site. Auto-generated captions: 'cap'/'capal'/'capow'/'Capital'/'Cap Tower' = Capitao throughout (fire turret area denial, punch-hole cash-door watch); 'oser'/'OA'/'Osa' = Osa (EMP + shields); 'aomi'/'a one ey'/'army' = the defender being flushed off Rafters, described as usually Amaru, sometimes Oryx or Jager — this is a Defense-side operator referenced only as the opponent being cleared, not an Attack pick, and is not listed in operatorRecommendations below. The 5th 'window/Claymore' player and the 'stock' player (suggested as 'a sledge or a gridlock') are described somewhat generically by role rather than a single fixed operator — flagged as flexible picks rather than hard-confirmed single operators. Compass directions added from R6 Trainer's labeled floor-plan blueprints (r6trainer.com), cross-referenced against the game's own compass-based room callouts; north is up on the map.

sites.workingName: CCTV / Cash (Garage / Cash / Construction / Top Red)

gadgetPlacements.location: South Wall of Bottom Garage, CC Window Side
gadgetPlacements.note: First hard-breach charge, placed before the other charge — avoids a defender popping out to frag the attacker mid-plant on the second charge.

gadgetPlacements.location: South Wall of Bottom Garage, Far Side
gadgetPlacements.note: Second hard-breach charge, used as the walk-in entrance to Bottom Garage. Watch for Wamai on this wall — if present, use a different player to clear it instead of walking straight in.
Destroy once the breaches are placed.
Opens the main breach into the site; also make shotgun head holes on this wall (the unreinforced side) to cover the Rafters angle and fire through the window onto the other side.

Walk into Bottom Garage once confirmed clear via the prep-phase drone, get underneath Rafters where the defender there can't see or kill you, and fire a Firebolt just past Panel 2 above the pipe/bolt — forces the defender to move toward Thermite's angle or the 90 side. Go in underneath, not through the Garage Door directly.
One shield covering one angle, a second on another; play behind both to stop anyone peeking Thermite while he holds the breach.
Throw an EMP pre-breach to counter a Bandit trick before Thermite opens it.

Smoke fired through the drone hole to cut off the crossfire angle from Top Red (a common tight-angle shotgun hold, e.g. Mute or Smoke) before crossing to plant. Wait for the defender to burn a magazine into the smoke before crossing.

gadgetPlacements.operator: Sledge, Gridlock, or similar flex operator
gadgetPlacements.note: Enter via Stock using the prep-phase vending-machine drone, hold Lobby all round to deny the C4-from-below run, and grenade/shoot through the Cash floor near the green-box corner as the Capitao push up Garage Stairs begins. Avoid nading directly above your own held position below.

gadgetPlacements.location: Cash-Door Punch Hole (after Rafters is taken)
gadgetPlacements.note: Once Rafters is cleared and taken, go prone at the head-hole wall and punch a hole to hold anyone entering from Construction or the Cash door.

gadgetPlacements.location: Window Platform (Opposite Side from Thermite/Osa Platform)
gadgetPlacements.note: Place one Claymore on each of the two windows, angled so a jumping defender is initially facing away from you. Stay tucked at the back of the platform out of sight from Construction/the window.

gadgetPlacements.location: Prep-Phase Drone Route: Rafters/Top Red/Bottom Garage
gadgetPlacements.note: Through the back of garage, up the stairs, across pipes near the duct, over a large pipe, dropped near Top Red/Rafters — gives vision of Top Red, Bottom Garage, and Rafters at once. Roughly 7-in-10 success; defenders sometimes jump through a window to contest early and catch the drone.
Hidden inside the vending machine near Stock — intel on Bottom Red and Bar door plus partial audio on Blue; also useful post-plant to watch Red Stairs.
Near the bottles/bar area for early intel on anyone passing through Bar or spawn-peeking the Bar window; not meant to stay there all round.

operatorRecommendations.role: Bottom Garage breach / Rafters clear / Cash-door hold
operatorRecommendations.note: Opens both Bottom Garage breach points, clears the garage camera, fire-flushes the Rafters defender into Thermite's kill angle, and later holds the Cash-door punch hole. Preferred over Grim here because the fire forces movement, whereas Grim's canister only pings a defender who stays put.
Opens the main wall, makes defender head holes for cover, holds the Rafters angle with Capitao's fire push, smokes the drone hole to cut off Top Red, and plants behind the bomb chassis.
EMPs the wall pre-breach, places two shields on the platform, and holds that door post-plant against Top Red or CC door pushes.
Enters via Stock, holds Lobby against C4-from-below, grenades/shoots through the Cash floor near the green box as Capitao pushes Garage Stairs.
Holds the opposite-side window platform with two angled Claymores to punish anyone jumping out toward the bomb after Capitao is dealt with.

generalTips.note: Clearing Rafters is the single most important task — the crossfire between Rafters and Top Red is the site's strongest defensive point. Once Rafters is cleared, the rest of the execute is close to guaranteed.
Works with as few as 2-3 players (minimum: Capitao/Rafters-clear plus Thermite/breach), comfortable with 3, full 5-player version (adds Osa, Stock lurker, window Claymore) is the ideal scenario.
Coordination between Capitao underneath Rafters and Thermite on the breach is essential — Thermite must be ready the instant the flushed defender breaks from behind Panel 2.

Post-plant, if the site was taken cleanly there's no threat from Top Red (Osa holds it), no C4 from below (Stock lurker holds Lobby), and no threat from Cash door/Construction (Capitao's punch hole) — the only remaining option is a very hard overhead C4 throw that tends not to stick.

Considered the weakest defensive site on Clubhouse despite being the most commonly played first by defenders — most attackers overcomplicate the Rafters clear by forcing it from Garage Door instead of going in underneath.
